items.q: How do you say 'I used the computer today'? (Spain Spanish)
items.why: In Spain, HOY (today) and recent time markers (esta semana, este mes) trigger PRETÉRITO PERFECTO (he + participle). Latin America uses preterite for these. Common error in essays: 'esta mañana fui' instead of 'esta mañana he ido'.

items.q: Which Spanish 'you' do you use with a stranger or elderly person?
items.why: Students use tú with everyone like English 'you'. Spanish distinguishes FORMAL (usted) for politeness/distance and INFORMAL (tú) for friends/family. Usted takes third-person verb forms, which trips students up.

items.q: Which mood follows 'Espero que' (I hope that)?
items.why: Students use indicative for what feels certain. Expressions of hope, wish, doubt, emotion + QUE trigger SUBJUNCTIVE: Espero que..., Quiero que..., Dudo que..., Es importante que... Common in future plans/aspirations writing.
Correct answer: 50. “15” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Spanish for age uses which verb?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Tengo (have). “Soy (am)” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.
Exact answer: 100. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
